channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: 'Pure theft': Trump allies can seek payouts from $2.5b 'lawfare' fund
item.description: The US has set up a fund to compensate Americans deemed victims of "lawfare". Democrats say it is "pure theft of public funds" to pay the president's allies.
item.dc:creator: Brad Ryan

media:group.media:description: Donald Trump spent days in court when he was prosecuted for various alleged crimes before returning to the White House.

item.title: Man dead, four injured in south-west Sydney shooting
item.description: A man has died and four others have been seriously injured following a Canley Heights shooting, which is believed to be organised crime-related.

media:group.media:description: A man has died and four others were injured in a shooting in Sydney's south-west.

item.title: Southampton expelled from Premier League playoffs for spying on rivals
item.description: Southampton loses its chance to gain promotion to the Premier League after being caught spying on Middlesbrough and two other rivals.

media:group.media:description: Southampton will not have the chance to gain promotion to the Premier League after unauthorised filming of Middlesbrough training sessions.

item.title: Six decades on and this Victorian community is still seeking its new bridge
item.description: Businesses relying on heavy transport to move goods across the Victoria-NSW border near Swan Hill say they are growing frustrated at the continual delays in replacing a 130-year-old wooden bridge.
item.dc:creator: Nethma Dandeniya

media:group.media:description: The Swan Hill Bridge was built in 1896 to accommodate a growing community.

item.title: San Diego mosque shooters met online, shared 'hatred'
item.description: Authorities have revealed the teenagers who killed three people at a San Diego mosque met online and shared a "broad hatred" toward different religions and races.

media:group.media:description: Three people were killed at the Islamic Center of San Diego.

item.title: Little-known weather phenomenon creates 'beautiful' sunsets up north
item.description: A "beautiful" optical illusion has been impacting sunsets in Australia's north, with a little-known weather phenomenon appearing to split golden hour into different coloured beams.
item.dc:creator: Gemma Ferguson

media:group.media:description: This sunset was photographed from a balcony in Muirhead, in Darwin.

item.title: Live: Coalition to unveil campaign to win over small businesses after budget
item.description: Shadow Treasurer Tim Wilson will today formally unveil a new opposition campaign he says will "back" the small businesses of Australia. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Boscaini

media:group.media:description: Shadow Minister for Industrial Relations, Employment and Small Business, Tim Wilson.

item.title: A mouse plague has broken out, here's what you need to know
item.description: Mice in their millions are eating grain crops, destroying food, and invading houses. Here's how it all started.
item.dc:creator: Joanna Prendergast

media:group.media:description: Farmers and others in the community are battling to clean up dead mice daily.

item.title: Former NT child protection staff speak out after Kumanjayi Little Baby case
item.description: In the three weeks since the alleged murder of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby in Alice Springs, the NT's child protection system has come under intense scrutiny. Now, former child protection workers are speaking out about why they believe the system is broken.
item.dc:creator: Alex Barwick and Olivana Lathouris

media:group.media:description: Peta-Lee Cole-Manolis has worked in senior child protection roles across the Northern Territory and Western Australia.

item.title: Arsenal ends 22-year Premier League drought after Man City draw
item.description: Arsenal's long wait for a Premier League title comes to an end as title challengers Manchester City drop points at Bournemouth.

media:group.media:description: Arsenal has won its first Premier League title since 2004 after three second-placed finishes under manager Mikel Arteta (front, right).

item.title: Death in custody highlights prison service delays, police union says
item.description: A man died while in the custody of Queensland's most crowded police watch house. The police union says the bottleneck is being caused by outdated prison service work practices.
item.dc:creator: James C Taylor

media:group.media:description: The police union says outdated work practices are causing record overcrowding in Queensland's watchhouses.

item.title: The young women chasing 'pure adrenaline' in motorsport drifting
item.description: A former track photographer, Georgia Tillyer bought her car, lovingly-named Night Fury, and learnt how to drive manual to enter her first event at Sydney Motorsport Park.
item.dc:creator: Tamsyn Buckle

media:group.media:description: Georgia Tillyer is often the only woman competing in drifting events.

item.title: UK police investigate sex abuse allegations linked to Epstein files
item.description: A British police force has said it is investigating two allegations of child sex abuse following information in documents relating to the late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

media:group.media:description: The Surrey Police force said it is investigating allegations of child sex abuse following information released in files relating to Jeffrey Epstein.

item.title: Green shoots after rain give SA farmers hope after years of drought
item.description: Farmers in the Riverland and Mallee are cautiously optimistic as new growth emerges from the paddock following "meaningful rain".
item.dc:creator: Eliza Berlage and Anita Ward

media:group.media:description: Green shoots give farmers hope after years of drought in South Australia's Mallee and Riverland regions.

item.title: Two dead after speeding car hits tree in Melbourne's south-east
item.description: Police say a car was travelling along the Princes Highway when the driver lost control and crashed into a tree about 1:20am.

media:group.media:description: Two people are dead after a car hit a tree on the Princes Highway in Dandenong.

item.title: The battle for Tasmania's north has been won, but locals aren't convinced
item.description: They may not be scheduled to play their first AFL game until 2028, but the Tasmania Devils have claimed their first scalp — booting Hawthorn out of the state which has been its second home for more than two decades.
item.dc:creator: Chris Rowbottom

media:group.media:description: Tasmania Devils players at first VFL game against Coburg at North Hobart oval, March 21, 2026.

item.title: Complaint from ex-boss led to investigation into embattled mental health clinic
item.description: Tasmania's largest private mental health hospital, which was given a $2 million lifeline by the state government last year, had its corporate governance placed under review, following a formal complaint by the facility's former chief executive.
item.dc:creator: Paddy Manning

media:group.media:description: An investigation into the Hobart Clinic's corporate governance was held following complaints from a former chief executive.

item.title: Warnings issued after surge in Queensland roadside close calls
item.description: Flashing lights on the roadside should mean one thing, but the RACQ says many drivers are not following their safety obligations.
item.dc:creator: Tobi Loftus

media:group.media:description: A car hits an RACQ van.

item.title: Why is there no vaccine for this Ebola outbreak?
item.description: The latest Ebola outbreak is caused by a strain of virus discovered nearly two decades ago, yet there is no vaccine. So what does this mean for people affected? And can vaccines be fast-tracked?
item.dc:creator: Jacinta Bowler

media:group.media:description: In past Ebola outbreaks, health authorities used temperature checks to try to limit the spread.

item.title: The men offering an alternative to ‘manosphere’ influencers
item.description: "Manosphere" rhetoric is getting louder online, but can men showcasing different lifestyles and values online cut through that noise?
item.dc:creator: Anna Chisholm

media:group.media:description: Caleb Ferdinand says he wants to be someone other people can relate to.

item.title: Luxury apartments greenlit under affordable housing scheme
item.description: Property developers are using an affordable housing scheme to bypass normal planning requirements and win fast-tracked approval for contentious projects across Melbourne.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Thompson

media:group.media:description: A luxury development in Hawthorn is among several high-end projects granted fast-tracked approval under the scheme.

item.title: Viral memes trying 'to catch fire on the internet' in AI Albo campaign
item.description: One startup founder who helped spread viral memes about capital gains tax reforms says the campaign was aimed at attracting attention after the budget, and not necessarily focused on accuracy.
item.dc:creator: Adelaide Miller

media:group.media:description: Frank Greeff shared this fake photo with Prime Minster Anthony Albanese to his Instagram account last week.

item.title: Vanuatu accuses Australia and China of 'undermining' it
item.description: Jotham Napat has confirmed Vanuatu has agreed to two pacts with both countries, but is not happy with either party.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Dziedzic

media:group.media:description: Jotham Napat said he was ready to sign the Nakamal Agreement with Anthony Albanese.

item.title: Life inside one of the world's most dangerous waterways
item.description: Before rising conflict pushed the Strait of Hormuz into the spotlight as a choke point vital to economies globally, tourism transformed women's lives.
item.dc:creator: Libby Hogan

media:group.media:description: Women of the Strait of Hormuz say they have been central to preserving traditional skills and culture on the islands.

item.title: Labor walks tightrope as tax overhaul alarms startups and investors
item.description: Labor MPs are gearing up for a hard sell on the impact of the federal government's tax changes on some small businesses, trusts and shares, despite many reporting "positive" feedback on housing reforms.
item.dc:creator: Clare Armstrong

media:group.media:description: The prime minister says the government is consulting on how to ensure tax changes do not disproportionately hurt startups and venture capital.
